---
title: "Quickstart: your first live cositos widget"
---

This page answers one question per language: **what is the shortest path to a live
cositos widget in my kernel, today?** For the full backend-maturity picture (why only
some languages are listed as "live" below) see the "Backend maturity" table in the root
`README.md` and the tier classification in `probe/README.md`.

Every snippet below renders the *same* counter widget — one anywidget-style ESM file,
reused verbatim, with **no new JavaScript** regardless of host language. The full
runnable notebooks these snippets are extracted from live in `examples/notebooks/`.

<!-- Repo-relative paths to files outside docs/ (README.md, probe/README.md,
     examples/notebooks/) are plain text here, not markdown links: docs/tutorials/*.qmd
     renders one directory level deeper than its source (docs/_site/tutorials/), so a
     relative link to a file outside docs/ 404s in the built site. See cositos-7co. -->

## Python

Live today (Tier 1, ipykernel). Full notebook: `examples/notebooks/python_counter.ipynb`.

Prerequisites: `cositos` importable in the kernel; `anywidget` installed in the
*frontend* (JupyterLab/Notebook) — cositos emits `_model_module="anywidget"`, so the
already-installed anywidget frontend renders it.

```{python}
# | eval: false
ESM = """
export default {
  render({ model, el }) {
    const btn = document.createElement("button");
    const out = document.createElement("span");
    const paint = () => { out.textContent = " count = " + model.get("count"); };
    btn.textContent = "increment";
    btn.addEventListener("click", () => {
      model.set("count", model.get("count") + 1);
      model.save_changes();
    });
    model.on("change:count", paint);
    paint();
    el.appendChild(btn); el.appendChild(out);
  }
}
"""

from cositos import Widget
from cositos.jupyter import CommTransport

# The host owns the state; cositos is the protocol glue (no observer magic).
state = {"_esm": ESM, "count": 0}
widget = Widget(
    CommTransport(),
    get_state=lambda: dict(state),
    set_state=state.update,  # inbound updates from the frontend land here
)
widget  # last line of a cell: renders live, opens the comm on first display
```

Click **increment** in the rendered widget, then read the count back from the kernel:
`state['count']`.

## Julia

Live today (Tier 1, IJulia). Full notebook: `examples/notebooks/julia_counter.ipynb`.

Prerequisites: an IJulia kernel bound to a project where `Cositos` and `IJulia` are
available; `anywidget` installed in the frontend (same reason as Python, above).

```julia
using Cositos, IJulia

ESM = raw"""
export default {
  render({ model, el }) {
    const btn = document.createElement("button");
    const out = document.createElement("span");
    const paint = () => { out.textContent = " count = " + model.get("count"); };
    btn.textContent = "increment";
    btn.addEventListener("click", () => {
      model.set("count", model.get("count") + 1);
      model.save_changes();
    });
    model.on("change:count", paint);
    paint();
    el.appendChild(btn); el.appendChild(out);
  }
}
"""

# The host owns the state; cositos is the protocol glue (no observer magic).
state = Dict{String,Any}("_esm" => ESM, "count" => 0)
widget = Widget(
    Cositos.ijulia_transport();
    get_state = () -> copy(state),
    set_state = (d) -> merge!(state, d),  # inbound updates from the frontend land here
);

widget  # last line of a cell: renders live, opens the comm on first display
```

Click **increment**, then read the count back: `state["count"]`.

## Clojure

Two live paths, deliberately different trade-offs:

- **Clay (recommended)** -- not a Jupyter kernel, but a full-duplex public websocket, no
  internal APIs. See [`docs/hosts.md`](../hosts.md#clay-claychannel) and
  `clojure -M:clay-notebook`.
- **clojupyter, via an internal-API crack** -- confirmed live (Tier 1, `cositos-059.9`),
  but reaches `clojupyter.state/current-context`, an internal, version-coupled
  implementation detail with no buffer or `custom`-message support. Full notebook:
  `examples/notebooks/clojure_counter.ipynb`.

```clojure
(require '[cemerick.pomegranate :as pom])
(pom/add-classpath "clojure/src")   ; cositos.core is not preinstalled on the kernel
(pom/add-classpath "clojure/dev")   ; cositos.clojupyter-transport ditto
(require '[cositos.clojupyter-transport :as tx] '[clojupyter.display :as display])

(def esm "export default { render({ model, el }) { /* ... same counter ... */ } }")

;; The host owns the state; cositos is the protocol glue (no observer magic).
(def state (atom {"_esm" esm "count" 0}))
(def transport (tx/open! @state))
(tx/on-update! transport (fn [new-state] (reset! state new-state)))

;; clojupyter has no auto-display hook, so rendering is one explicit call:
(display/render-mime tx/widget-view-mimetype (tx/widget-view transport))
```

Click **increment**, then read the count back: `(get @state "count")`.

## R, C# -- not yet available

These two backends are **protocol-core only**: the message-building and
buffer-split/merge logic is ported and fixture-certified, but there is no live-widget
path yet because the *kernel's* comm support is blocked upstream -- not a gap in
cositos itself. See `probe/README.md` for how each was classified.

| Language | Blocked on | Tracking |
|---|---|---|
| R | IRkernel 1.3.2's `comm$open()` throws an internal `send_response` arity error | `cositos-059.4` |
| C# | .NET Interactive doesn't answer `comm_info_request` -- it uses a bespoke kernel protocol, not the standard ipywidgets comm surface | `cositos-059.6` |

If you're evaluating a language for a polyglot team today, treat this table as
current status, not a roadmap commitment -- each is blocked on an upstream kernel fix,
not on cositos work.

## Next steps

- Author a new widget's ESM: `tutorials/authoring-widgets.qmd`.
- Embed a widget into an existing tool (Quarto, JupyterBook, Voila, Clay, …):
  `tutorials/integrating.qmd`.
